parameters.name: glslversion
parameters.label: GLSL Version
parameters.description: Pick what version of GLSL to compile the shader with.

parameters.name: mode
parameters.label: Mode
parameters.description: Choose what type of shader you are writing, vertex/pixel shader, or a compute shader.

parameters.name: vertexdat
parameters.label: Vertex Shader
parameters.description: Points to the holding the . Drag & Drop a here, or manually enter the path to the .

parameters.name: pixeldat
parameters.label: Pixel Shader
parameters.description: Points to the holding the Pixel . Drag & Drop a here, or manually enter the path to the .

parameters.name: computedat
parameters.label: Compute Shader
parameters.description: Points to the holding the . Drag & Drop a here, or manually enter the path to the .

parameters.name: loaduniformnames
parameters.label: Load Uniform Names
parameters.description: When this button is pressed the node will try to pre-fill all it's uniform parameter with uniforms that are declare in the shader. Note that the shader compiler will likely not expose uniforms that ar

parameters.name: autodispatchsize
parameters.label: Auto Dispatch Size
parameters.description: Automatically set the dispatch size based on the compute shader's local size and the output texture resolution. Ensures at least one thread per pixel will execute.

parameters.name: outputaccess
parameters.label: Output Access
parameters.description: Controls how the output textures will be accessed. If the textures will be read from (such as using previous frame's values), then the access should be changed to Read-Write instead of Write Only.

parameters.name: type
parameters.label: Output Type
parameters.description: Specify what type of texture to create. When creating  a 3D texture the will render once for every slice of the output. Refer to for more info.

parameters.name: depth
parameters.label: Depth
parameters.description: Set the depth of the 3D texture from the or the parameter.

parameters.name: customdepth
parameters.label: Custom Depth
parameters.description: Manually set the depth of the 3D texture, otherwise it will use the depth of the input.

parameters.name: inputmapping
parameters.label: Input Mapping
parameters.description: Determines how the node's input(s) are passed into the shader for use when creating a 3D Texture. By default all of the inputs are passed to each slice. When using the mode, the first N inputs are pas

parameters.name: nval
parameters.label: N Value
parameters.description: Determines how many inputs are passed to the shader per slice when using the mode for . If for example this is set to 2, then the first 2 inputs will be passed to the first slice, the next 2 inputs wi

parameters.name: inputextenduv
parameters.label: Input Extend Mode UV
parameters.description: Controls what is returned from your texture sampling functions when the U and V texture coordinates (called S and T in the shader) are outside [0-1] range.

parameters.name: inputextendw
parameters.label: Input Extend Mode W
parameters.description: Controls what is returned from your texture sampling functions when the W texture coordinate (called W in the shader) are outside [0-1] range. Only useful for .

parameters.name: numcolorbufs
parameters.label: # of Color Buffers
parameters.description: Any shader you write can output to more than one RGBA buffer at a time. Turn up this value to have more color buffers allocated for you, and refer to [Write_a_GLSL_ #Outputting_to_Multiple_Color_Buffe

parameters.name: array0name

parameters.label: Type
parameters.description: The data type of the uniform in the shader.

parameters.name: array0chop

parameters.description: The channels from this will be sent to the GLSL shader.
